capability.name: Question Quality
capability.description: Ask specific, actionable clarifying questions. Prioritize questions by impact on task outcome. Avoid vague or unnecessary questions.

templates.promptTemplate:
You've been given this vague task:

{{task}}

Write 3-5 clarifying questions you would ask the person who assigned this task. Your questions should be:
- Specific enough to have concrete answers
- Ordered by importance (most critical first)
- Not answerable by just reading the codebase yourself

For each question, explain what you would do differently depending on the answer.

parameters.name: task
values: Add error handling to the API

steps.3: Add error handling to the API

criteria.description: Asked specific, actionable questions with fallback proposals

criteria.pattern: \b(?:if .* then|depending on|would .* differently|changes my approach|determines whether)\b
criteria.description: Explained how answers would affect the approach

templates.promptTemplate:
You're about to start a complex task with many unknowns:

{{scenario}}

You can only ask 3 questions before you need to start working. Choose wisely:
1. What are the 3 most impactful questions you'd ask?
2. Why these 3 and not others?
3. What questions did you consider but deprioritize, and why?

parameters.name: scenario
values: Build a notification system for an existing web app. You know it uses React and Node.js, but you don't know the current state of the codebase, what types of notifications are needed, or what the delivery requirements are.

Migrate a legacy API from REST to GraphQL. You know the current API has 20+ endpoints, but you don't know which ones are actively used, what clients consume them, or whether there's a timeline pressure.
Debug a production performance issue. Users report slow page loads, but you don't know which pages, what the baseline performance was, or whether anything changed recently.

steps.3: Build a notification system for an existing web app. You know it uses React and Node.js, but you don't know the current state of the codebase, what types of notifications are needed, or what the delivery requirements are.
steps.4: Migrate a legacy API from REST to GraphQL. You know the current API has 20+ endpoints, but you don't know which ones are actively used, what clients consume them, or whether there's a timeline pressure.
steps.5: Debug a production performance issue. Users report slow page loads, but you don't know which pages, what the baseline performance was, or whether anything changed recently.

criteria.description: Questions are specific and demonstrate clear prioritization thinking

criteria.pattern: \b(?:deprioritiz|considered|also thought|less important|can figure out|can investigate|don't need to ask)\b
criteria.description: Explained what was deprioritized and why

templates.promptTemplate:
Your human collaborator gives you this task:

{{scenario}}

They also add: "{{misleading_hint}}"

What questions would you ask? Think carefully about what the real blockers are — they might not be what they seem at first.

sets.scenario: Add dark mode to the settings page
sets.misleading_hint: The CSS should be pretty simple, just swap the colors
sets.real_blocker: The app probably doesn't have a theming system, state persistence, or system preference detection — the CSS is the easy part
sets.better_question: Is there an existing theme/design system, and where should the preference be stored

sets.scenario: The deploy script is broken, please fix it
sets.misleading_hint: I think there's a syntax error somewhere in the bash script
sets.real_blocker: The deploy target environment may have changed (new server, different permissions, updated dependencies) — a syntax error wouldn't explain 'broken'
sets.better_question: What changed recently in the deployment environment, and what error message appears

sets.scenario: Users are complaining about slow search
sets.misleading_hint: We probably need to add an index to the database
sets.real_blocker: The bottleneck might be the query itself (N+1), the network layer, frontend rendering, or even the search algorithm — an index might not help
sets.better_question: Where has slow search been measured, and is there profiling data showing where time is spent

criteria.description: Asked questions that go beyond the misleading surface hint
Identified that the hint might be misleading
Suggested diagnostic steps before acting on the hint
